description: TCG Vendor ID Registry v1.06 (2024) with enriched metadata. Each entry: {id, name, kind, platforms, ek_root_ca_source, ek_subject_pattern, product_families, ek_ca_thumbprints, known_cves, notes}. Keys are the 4-byte ASCII vendor ID hex-encoded (same form as the `tpmManufacturer` TCG OID 2.23.133.2.1 value on the EK cert, with the `id:` prefix stripped). Look up this table to render a human-readable vendor name and derive EK-chain-validation metadata.
source: TCG Vendor ID Registry v1.06 (2024); vendor PKI pages (Infineon / Nuvoton / ST / AMD / Intel / Microsoft / Google / Lenovo / HP); Linux kernel drivers/char/tpm/tpm-chip.c; NIST NVD for CVE references.

414D4400.name: AMD
414D4400.ek_cert_acquisition: sha256(0x00002222 || exp || modulus)[0..16] as the hash key
414D4400.notes: fTPM runs inside the AMD PSP firmware. Compromise of the PSP compromises the TPM.

414E5400.name: Ant Group
414E5400.notes: TCG vendor code ANT. New in TCG VID Registry v1.06 (2024).

41544D4C.name: Atmel / Microchip
41544D4C.ek_root_ca_source: Microchip support portal (Atmel acquired by Microchip Technology in 2016)

4252434D.name: Broadcom
4252434D.notes: BCM0102 series. Rare in new products.

4353434F.name: Cisco
4353434F.notes: Cisco UCS platforms ship TPMs identified as Cisco rather than the chip vendor.

464C5953.name: Flyslice Technologies
464C5953.notes: FLYS. Small vendor; rarely encountered outside mainland China.

474F4F47.name: Google
474F4F47.ek_root_ca_source: Google publishes the GCE + Chromebook EK root CA chain at https://pki.goog/; Cr50/Ti50 per-device certs via chromium.org attestation tools.
474F4F47.notes: Google fTPM variants: (1) Cr50/Ti50 in Chromebooks/Pixels — true hardware isolated fTPM; (2) GCE Shielded VM vTPM — software TPM running on hypervisor. GCE vTPMs emit a UTF-16LE CRTM of "GCE Virtual Firmware v2".

48495349.name: HiSilicon (Huawei)
48495349.notes: HiSilicon is Huawei in-house. Kunpeng servers integrate an fTPM in the processor SoC.

48505100.name: HP Inc.
48505100.ek_root_ca_source: HP Inc. TPM 2.0 EK Root CA, distributed via HP TPM Provisioning tool and HP driver portal
48505100.notes: HP Inc. (consumer + commercial client). Distinct from HPE (servers). Uses Infineon/STM/Nuvoton chips underneath; but EK is re-issued under an HP-owned intermediate.

49424D00.name: IBM
49424D00.notes: IBM-identified TPMs on modern Power systems. ThinkPad TPMs after the Lenovo acquisition report as Lenovo.

49465800.name: Infineon
49465800.ek_subject_pattern: CN=Infineon OPTIGA(TM) TPM 2.0 RSA CA NNN, OU=OPTIGA(TM), O=Infineon Technologies AG, C=DE
known_cves: CVE-2017-15361 (ROCA weak RSA key generation, affects SLB 9635 and earlier)
49465800.notes: Largest share of enterprise/laptop discrete TPM chips. Affected by the 2017 ROCA vulnerability if not firmware-updated.

494E5443.name: Intel
platforms: Core 6th-gen+ (PTT enabled by default on most)
494E5443.ek_root_ca_source: 11th-gen+: EK cert lives in TPM NV at 0x01C00100+; pre-11th-gen: https://ekop.intel.com/ekcertservice/WellKnownRoot/
known_cves: CVE-2019-16863 / CVE-2019-11090 (TPM-FAIL timing leak on Intel ME fTPM)
494E5443.notes: Intel Platform Trust Technology runs inside the Management Engine (CSME). PTT since Skylake (6th gen). 11th gen+ keeps EK cert NV-resident.

494E5458.name: InteXX
494E5458.notes: Allocated in TCG VID Registry; very few devices observed.

4C454E00.name: Lenovo
4C454E00.ek_root_ca_source: https://pki.lenovo.com/ (Lenovo ThinkPad TPM Root CA + Lenovo ThinkSystem TPM Root CA)
4C454E00.notes: Lenovo re-issues the underlying chip-vendor EK under a Lenovo intermediate. CRTM is UTF-16LE with 3-char prefix disambiguating generation (see firmware-versions/lenovo-thinkpad.json).

4D534654.name: Microsoft
4D534654.notes: Software TPM used by Hyper-V + Azure.

4E534D20.name: NSING
4E534D20.notes: New in TCG VID Registry v1.06 (2024).

4E544300.name: Nuvoton
known_cves: CVE-2023-34440 (Nuvoton NPCT75x ECDSA leaked scalar bit)
4E544300.notes: Strong adoption in consumer + embedded. Framework laptops use NPCT7xx.

4E545A00.name: Nationz
4E545A00.notes: Nationz Technologies Inc. (Shenzhen). Major vendor in the Chinese domestic ecosystem.

51434F4D.name: Qualcomm
platforms: Windows on Arm laptops (Surface Pro X, Galaxy Book4 Edge)
51434F4D.notes: Qualcomm fTPM inside the Snapdragon Security Processing Unit.

524F4343.name: Rockchip
platforms: Rockchip RK3399 / RK3588 (Linux SBCs: PineBook, Radxa)
524F4343.notes: Rockchip fTPM via the SoC Secure-Enclave (BootROM + TrustZone).

534D4643.name: SMSC
534D4643.notes: SMSC was acquired by Microchip in 2012. Rarely encountered in modern products.

534D4E4E.name: Samsung
534D4E4E.notes: Samsung Exynos fTPM inside the Shannon baseband.

53544D20.name: STMicroelectronics
53544D20.notes: Second-largest discrete vendor after Infineon. ST33TPHF20X common in PC-client.

54584E00.name: Texas Instruments
54584E00.notes: TXN — Texas Instruments TPMs. Limited adoption.

57454300.name: Winbond / Nuvoton (legacy)
57454300.notes: Older devices may still report Winbond (WEC).

53656345.name: SecEdge
53656345.notes: New in TCG VID Registry v1.06 (2024).

534F4C49.name: Solidigm
platforms: Solidigm SSDs with TCG Opal self-encrypting drive TPM-like attestation
534F4C49.notes: New in TCG VID Registry v1.06 (2024). Former Intel memory business.

unknown.name: Unknown / Unregistered
unknown.notes: Vendor ID not in the TCG Vendor ID Registry. Could indicate a pre-release TPM, custom-fab chip, or corrupted cert.
